name: Remotely Operated Vehicle
description: Designs and builds underwater robots for the MATE International ROV Competition. Placed 6th at 2024 Worlds.
longDescription: The Purdue IEEE ROV (Remotely Operated Vehicle) committee is a world-class underwater robotics team that competes annually in the MATE International ROV Competition. Our mission is to design, manufacture, and operate advanced underwater robots capable of performing complex tasks in extreme environments. The committee is divided into four main subteams—Mechanical, Electrical, Software, and Administrative—working together to solve challenges in sensor fusion, real-time control, and fluid dynamics. Our most recent vehicle, X15 ISO-Squid, secured 6th place globally at the 2024 World Championships.

joinConfig.message: We accept new members at the beginning of each Fall semester. Visit us at the callout!

sections.title: Subteams
sections.content: Our team is structured to simulate a real engineering firm. The **Mechanical** subteam handles structural design and waterproofing. **Electrical** designs custom PCBs and power systems. **Software** develops the control logic and pilot interface. Finally, our **Administrative** subteam manages the business and documentation aspects of the competition.

items.name: X15 ISO-Squid
items.description: Our 2024 competition vehicle. Features a 6-DOF control system, custom gripper, and high-resolution video telemetry.

items.name: X14 Calamari
items.description: The 2023 vehicle that pioneered our modular electronics housing and custom thruster designs.

items.question: When can I join?
items.answer: We typically recruit new members at the start of the Fall semester during our annual callout.

items.question: What is the time commitment?
items.answer: Active members typically spend 5-10 hours per week in the lab, with more hours during the weeks leading up to competition.
